results.title: Kintsugi (Wikipedia)
results.snippet: Source-domain: Japanese golden-joinery repair; visible imperfection embraced.
results.kw_notes: Individual tokens (kintsugi, repair, visible, imperfection) present, but full multi-word content_words ('kintsugi repair', 'wabi-sabi imperfection', 'visible joint') not present as substrings.
results.judge_reason: Canonical source-domain explainer for kintsugi golden-joinery and visible imperfection; strongly relevant to source side.

results.title: Kintsugi (Britannica)
results.snippet: Source-domain: history of kintsugi pottery repair.
results.kw_notes: 'kintsugi' and 'repair' co-occur but not as exact substring 'kintsugi repair'; lenient kw=1 via near-adjacent tokens, strict kw=0.
results.judge_reason: Encyclopedia coverage of kintsugi pottery repair history — squarely on source-domain.

results.title: The Art of Imperfection: Kintsugi Pottery, Wabi-Sabi (Japan tourism)
results.kw_notes: 'wabi-sabi imperfection' appears in snippet; 'kintsugi' present (partial of 'kintsugi repair').
results.judge_reason: Directly covers wabi-sabi+kintsugi pairing, the conceptual heart of the source domain.

results.title: Unlearning Isn't Invisible: Detecting Unlearning Traces in LLMs
results.snippet: Unlearning leaves persistent fingerprints in LLM behavior + internal representations.
results.kw_notes: No content_word substring present; 'traces' related to 'error trace annotation' but not substring match.
results.judge_reason: Closest LLM-side prior: detectable but unlabeled unlearning traces — direct foil for the kintsugi proposal of deliberate visible inscription.

results.title: Why Do Multi-Agent LLM Systems Fail? (MAST taxonomy)
results.snippet: MAST taxonomy + dataset of 1600+ annotated multi-agent failure traces.
results.kw_notes: 'annotated' + 'traces' tokens overlap with 'error trace annotation' conceptually but not as substring.
results.judge_reason: Failure-trace taxonomy is adjacent prior art but does not propose visible-inscription correction.

results.title: Demystifying Errors in LLM Reasoning Traces (code execution simulation)
results.snippet: Structured taxonomy of reasoning errors in code-inference tasks.
results.kw_notes: No substring hit; error/trace tokens present individually.
results.judge_reason: Reasoning-error taxonomy; descriptive not prescriptive about visible repair.

results.title: Large Language Model Reasoning Failures
results.snippet: Reasoning failures classified along three types: fundamental architectural, application-specific, robustness.
results.judge_reason: Generic failure taxonomy; weak overlap with explicit-inscription idea.

results.title: From Backward Spreading to Forward Replay: Target Construction in LLM Parameter Editing
results.kw_notes: 'post-hoc' present; 'correction' implied by 'editing' but not substring — lenient kw=1, strict kw=0.
results.judge_reason: Post-hoc model-editing prior; relevant alternative approach but does not annotate edits visibly.

summary.comment: Primary suspects PASS: the three source-domain results hit, and the four LLM-side priors (2506.14003, 2503.13657, 2512.00215, 2605.00358) are correctly cited as adjacent-but-distinct. However the PASS hides a critical retrieval gap: audit-log / chain-of-edit / git-diff-style overlap was NOT searched or retrieved. Mature literatures on (a) provenance/audit-log annotations for LLM outputs, (b) chain-of-edit / chain-of-thought-with-revision visible-revision traces, and (c) diff-style edit-history rendering in LLM agents (e.g. Aider/Cursor diff blocks, revision-trace papers, watermark+attribution traces) directly anticipate the 'visible inscription of corrections' core of the kintsugi mapping. None of these is in 06_search_raw.json, so the novelty_claim is under-tested. Recommend a third query targeting 'LLM output audit log chain-of-edit visible revision trace diff annotation' before accepting the novelty claim.
